what_it_does: Declares a planned desktop surface specification (D5) for the J8 journey, defining what it represents, which ui:// addresses it surfaces, and what build artifacts and checks it targets.
observations: It is a generated surface-spec, not a hand-authored design file.
D5 is scoped to the J8 journey with no cross-journey references.
It represents three related concepts: SM-node, SM-panel, SM-extension.
It lists six ui:// addresses that must exist and must not be invented.
Build targets include components (grow, inspector, palette, workshop), a design lint command, and a token-to-CSS pipeline.
Status is planned and the platform is desktop only.
standouts: The address list mixes a primary grow path with inspector, palette, and workshop addresses.
The build_to block explicitly ties the surface to a lint gate and a single token source.
It documents its own generation context and a rule against inventing addresses.
conventions: Follows a structured surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, journeys, addresses, and build_to.
References addresses with a ui:// scheme and maps them to component names.
concerns: No cross_journeys are listed, which may be intentional or may indicate incomplete wiring.
Status planned means the spec is not yet implemented.
The design_lint command is given as a shell-like string without full path context.
notes: This is the planned desktop surface D5 for J8, listing the addresses and build targets it expects to surface.
questions: What do SM-node, SM-panel, and SM-extension specifically denote?
Which exact component implements each ui:// address?
How does the design_lint command integrate with the build pipeline?
Why are there no cross-journey references for a surface that spans node/panel/extension concepts?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a generated surface spec for view D5; the file matches that purpose by declaring identifiers, addresses, and build targets.
apparent_themes: Build-time verification and single-source design tokens.
intention_signals: It tries to establish a verifiable, build-linked definition of a desktop grow/inspector/palette/workshop surface so that planned UI work can be traced to concrete addresses, components, and quality gates.
novelty: The spec self-documents its generation rule ('never invent an address') and binds surface addresses to a lint gate plus a single token source.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, address-anchored plan for a J8 desktop surface and records which components, lint checks, and token pipeline are expected to realize it.
summary_for_embedding: D5.json is a planned desktop surface specification for J8 that lists represented concepts, ui:// addresses, target components, a design lint gate, and a token-to-CSS source.
intention_for_embedding: It was generated to provide a build-linked, address-driven plan for the grow/inspector/palette/workshop surface in the J8 journey.
